role_title: Lead Product Manager, Agentic Ads Platform

level_signals: Zero-to-one track record required, ideally at B2B SaaS
High-visibility role working directly with founders and executive team
Expected to co-own pipeline and go-to-market alongside engineering and design

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have built products from nothing to meaningful revenue, ideally at a B2B SaaS company. The domain matters less than evidence you can find product-market fit under uncertainty.

You can evaluate architectural tradeoffs, reason about things like multi-model orchestration and API integration design at the level of product decisions, and hold your own in debates with senior engineers.
You think in terms of pipeline, conversion, and expansion. You build roadmaps that map to revenue targets.
Small team, high autonomy, tight cycles. We use AI heavily in our own workflows and expect you to do the same.
This role spans engineering, design, sales, CS, and leadership. You drive clarity from ambiguity.
Run deep customer discovery with performance marketing and creative teams at enterprise companies. Understand their workflows, pain points, and what would earn their trust.
Define the product roadmap tied to revenue milestones. Sequence what to build, what to compose from existing systems, and what to partner on.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: ideally at a B2B SaaS company

scope.surface: Agentic Ads Platform — AI-driven advertising workflow covering creative generation, campaign management, and performance optimization across Meta, Google, TikTok, LinkedIn, Snapchat
core_responsibilities: Lead the Agentic Ads Platform product area
Run deep customer discovery with performance marketing and creative teams at enterprise companies
Define the product roadmap tied to revenue milestones
Ship product working directly with engineering and design on tight cycles
Co-own pipeline and go-to-market: join customer calls, shape competitive positioning, partner with CS on implementation
Drive multi-model creative generation roadmap (video, motion graphics, multi-model orchestration)
Improve marketing context and brand adherence for enterprise creative teams
Deepen platform integrations across Meta, Google, TikTok, LinkedIn, Snapchat
Define the agentic creation experience (conversational interface, review/approval workflows, progressive autonomy controls)

green_flags: Clear product surface with live customers already generating revenue
Strong mission-driven narrative with specific enterprise logos (Domino's, Spotify, Chime, Ramp, etc.)
High-autonomy, high-visibility role reporting directly to founders and exec team
Product area is differentiated with hard-to-replicate moat (warehouse-native data + agent orchestration)
Explicit acknowledgment of fast-moving market with strong demand signals
red_flags: Location not specified anywhere in the JD — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite
E-Verify participation and no mention of visa sponsorship strongly suggests no sponsorship available, but this is inferred not stated
High buzzword density may obscure actual day-to-day expectations
No years-of-experience requirement stated, making seniority calibration difficult

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'Lead' title, founder-level visibility, zero-to-one requirement)
location.type (not stated anywhere in JD; marked unclear)
location.visa_sponsorship (inferred as 'no' from E-Verify statement and absence of sponsorship language)
location.work_auth (inferred as no-sponsorship from E-Verify participation)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer examples)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated explicitly)
ambiguities: Location is entirely unstated — the JD contains no city, remote, or hybrid information. Marked as 'unclear'.
E-Verify statement implies US-based hiring and no visa sponsorship, but the JD does not explicitly state 'no sponsorship' — treated as no-sponsorship based on strong implication.
The nice-to-have 'ideally at a B2B SaaS company' appears within a must-have requirement sentence; the broader requirement (zero-to-one track record) is treated as hard, the B2B SaaS context as soft.
